summary: Implemented minimal M1 GameCommand v1, DomainEvent/StateDelta outputs, Query v1, protocol parsers, and a shared sim-core boot/preview/submit/query canary used by both Node runner and Worker-compatible adapter. Player, AI, and system actors use the same command union, validation, apply path, and result shape.

tests_run.result: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 26 passed (26). Includes SIM-003 command/query and Node/Worker contract tests. Node warning about MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts is pre-existing test environment noise.
Node hash: 61e0ba1c; Worker-compatible hash: 61e0ba1c; Node command/query hash: d275faa4; Worker-compatible command/query hash: d275faa4; Deterministic command/query simulation matched.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 15 expected violation(s).
architecture:check and sim:determinism passed; root Vitest Test Files 8 passed (8), Tests 32 passed (32); web Vitest Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3).
pnpm -r --if-present typecheck completed; web, sim-core, desktop, client-core, map-renderer, ui, platform, core, and tools checks passed.
ESLint completed with no reported errors.
All matched files use Prettier code style.

decisions: Kept SIM-003 within ADR-004: explicit commands produce semantic events/deltas, but no full event sourcing was introduced.
Used minimal M1 commands only: sim.advance-day, debug.set-district-control, and sim.verify-state-hash.
Kept duplicate/replay tracking in SimulationRuntimeV1 acceptedCommandIds for M1 runtime authority; WorldState v0 schema was not expanded in this task.
Worker-compatible adapter delegates to shared sim-core canary execution instead of duplicating validation or apply logic.
Query v1 returns read-model DTOs for state hash, calendar, and district summaries; it does not expose WorldState internals.
risks: R3: command/protocol/authority behavior changed and requires systems_architect review.
Duplicate command detection is runtime-scoped in M1 and not yet persisted in save/replay tail; this should be revisited with snapshot/save v1 work.
Current command parser is hand-written because no production schema dependency is approved; future broader protocol surface may need a reviewed schema strategy.
requested_action: Review SIM-003 for deterministic command semantics, authority boundary, protocol/query shape, and Node/Worker hash parity. Do not mark ACCEPTED without independent systems review.
